title: Folding Plant Stand
summary: A three-shelf plant stand with bolted, pivoting joints — the shelves fold flat against the frame for storage instead of staying open year-round.
description:
Unlike a fixed plant stand, this one is built to fold: the shelves pivot on bolts rather than screws, so the whole thing collapses flat when you don't need it and pops back open for the growing season. That means every pivot point in this build is a bolted joint, not a fastened one — get the hole locations and diameters consistent across matching parts, or the shelves won't swing evenly.

Assemble the frame first and level it before drilling the pivot holes for the shelves; the plan has you clamp a level against the shelf tops and drill through both the shelf and the leg assembly at once, which is what keeps every shelf sitting flat once it's folded open, rather than guessing at hole placement on each piece separately.

tools.note: Used to set the shelves level while drilling their pivot holes.
Holding the shelf and frame together while drilling matched pivot holes.

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 30° off square, ends parallel.
Short point to short point, both ends 30° off square, not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends 30° off square, not parallel.

steps.title: Cut and drill the leg boards
steps.body: Cut all boards to size, with the leg boards cut at their angled ends as specified. Drill pivot holes in the leg boards as laid out — you'll only need the bottom set of holes on two of the four leg boards.

steps.title: Assemble the leg pairs
steps.body: Attach the two leg boards together in pairs with 2" fasteners. If screwing, predrill carefully; if nailing, use plenty of exterior-rated glue.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf supports
steps.body: Measure carefully and attach the bottom shelf supports with 2" screws, overhanging 3/4" from the legs on each side.

steps.title: Bolt the bottom pivot joint
steps.body: Drill 1/4" holes in the bottom shelf support board. Attach the back leg and bottom shelf support together with a bolt, using three washers — one on each end and one between the boards to protect the finish from wear. Set this assembly aside.

steps.title: Attach the end supports
steps.body: Attach the shelf end supports with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Drill the shelf pivot holes and bolt everything together
steps.body: Set a level across the top of the shelves as you clamp them onto the frame, and drill matched 1/4" holes through both the shelf and the leg/support assembly at once — drilling them together, rather than separately, is what keeps every shelf sitting flat once folded open. Attach with bolts so the shelves can pivot freely, checking that the shelf tops are level before you fully tighten each one.

steps.title: Test the fold
steps.body: Fold the shelves flat against the frame and back open a few times to confirm everything pivots smoothly before loading it with plants.

images.alt: A three-shelf wooden plant stand with bolted, folding pivot joints
